title: Implement MeshCore node identity, status, and config read handlers
description: Why this exists: after connecting, a MeshCore client should see a coherent local LICHEN node. What needs to be done: implement supported read handlers for node identity, firmware/app version, radio/local status, device capabilities, battery/power placeholders, position/time placeholders, channel/config readbacks, and adapter limitations. Map values from LICHEN HAL and local status resources where available. Acceptance criteria: client can read supported identity/status/config data without hangs; synthetic or unavailable fields are explicit; unsupported writes/admin operations are rejected deterministically; native_sim tests validate encoded responses.
notes:
Input from project-LICHEN-9c4y.2 contract: DEVICE_INFO/SELF_INFO/channel/contact/status reads should use HAL identity/capability/status plus compatibility-local state. Stable MeshCore public-key identity remains blocked on project-LICHEN-9c4y.14; channel/config secret policy remains blocked on project-LICHEN-9c4y.17.
Input from project-LICHEN-9c4y.14 identity projection: SELF_INFO should expose the LICHEN Ed25519 public key byte-for-byte as MeshCore public key once a shared public identity provider exists. If no public key provider is initialized, identity-bearing reads should return BAD_STATE or explicitly degraded read-only fields; they must not invent a real-messageable synthetic key. project-LICHEN-uzom tracks the required provider.
